_grading.operationalization: FIRED := the pptx Skill tool is invoked in the fresh-session stream-json transcript (directly observed tool_use, name=Skill, skill=pptx). SILENT := no such invocation. Behavioral signature recorded as corroboration, not the primary gate.
_grading.fire_signature: The session builds slides with python-pptx: layouts chosen from the master (template surveyed when one exists), text in placeholders not free textboxes, charts as native chart parts via CategoryChartData + add_chart (never a pasted image), and the saved deck reopened to assert slide count / chart presence before delivery.
_grading.silent_signature: For near-miss prompts the session routes elsewhere: prose documents go to docx, spreadsheet analysis goes to xlsx, PDF reading goes to pdf. No pptx-skill load and no slide machinery on deliverables that are not decks.
_grading.surface: Claude Code headless (claude -p), project-scope install in this repo
_grading.gate: >=3/4 should-fire runs fire; >=2/3 should-not runs stay silent; a fire that then ships a chart as an image, or bullets as free textboxes, counts as a failure even if the skill loaded
_grading.gate_note: 3/4-fire and 2/3-silent are the small-N analogues of the governors' >=5/6 / >=3/4 convention (rate thresholds per architecture-contract invariant-3 scope note); measured rates get recorded as rates.

evals.prompt: Make a deck out of this: quarterly revenue by region is in sales.csv — board-ready, a few slides, one chart.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pptx loads; CSV aggregated, then the section-header -> chart-slide -> takeaway-bullets trio emitted with a native COLUMN/BAR chart from CategoryChartData; deck reopened and slide count + has_chart asserted before delivery.

evals.prompt: Turn these meeting notes into a presentation — 4 or 5 slides with a title slide and bullet-point takeaways: notes.md
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pptx loads; slides built on real layouts (Title Slide, Title and Content) with text in placeholders and bullet levels via text_frame paragraphs — not a doc chopped into pages of textboxes.

evals.prompt: Add a chart slide to deck.pptx showing these numbers: East 412, West 519, Midwest 337. Keep the existing template look.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pptx loads; Presentation('deck.pptx') opened so the existing masters/theme are inherited, layouts surveyed by name, and a native editable chart added via chart_data + add_chart — never a pasted image of a chart.

evals.prompt: Put these numbers into a few slides for tomorrow's standup: signups 1.2k, churn 3.1%, NPS 41.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pptx loads; a short deck on default or existing layouts with placeholders; proportionality respected — three simple slides, no heavy chart machinery unless a chart genuinely helps.

evals.prompt: Write up the Q2 results as a two-page Word report with headings and a summary table.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): a prose report is docx territory (real Word styles, tables); no pptx load, no slide machinery on a paged document.

evals.prompt: Take sales.csv and build me a workbook with per-region totals, a monthly pivot, and formulas I can audit.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): the analysis belongs in a spreadsheet — xlsx owns it (openpyxl workbook, formulas). pptx only joins later if the user asks to present the results as a deck.

evals.prompt: What do the slides in pitch.pdf say about pricing? Pull out the relevant pages.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): reading an existing PDF — even one that renders slides — is pdf's job (pdfplumber/pypdf extraction with page citations); no pptx load.
